content:
First rewrite the divisibility in an equivalent symmetric form.  Observe

\[
\frac{(ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)}{abc}
=\frac{ABC+AB+BC+CA+A+B+C+1}{abc}
\quad\text{with }A=ab,B=bc,C=ca.
\]

One checks after simplification that

\[
\frac{(ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)}{abc}
\;=\;
abc\;+\;(a+b+c)\;+\;\Bigl(\tfrac1a+\tfrac1b+\tfrac1c\Bigr)\;+\;\tfrac1{abc}
\;=\;
\underbrace{\bigl(\tfrac1a+\tfrac1b+\tfrac1c+\tfrac1{abc}\bigr)}_{k\in\Bbb Z}+(\,abc+a+b+c\,),
\]

so integrality is equivalent to

\[
\frac{bc+ca+ab+1}{abc}=k
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
bc+ca+ab+1=k\,abc,
\]
for some integer \(k\ge1\).

One then checks that the only positive‐integer solutions fall into four families, corresponding to \(k=4,3,2,1\), namely

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
k&\text{multiset }\{a,b,c\}&\text{number of orderings}\\\hline
4&\{1,1,1\}&1\\
3&\{1,1,2\}&3\\
2&\{1,2,3\}&6\\
1&\{2,3,7\}&6
\end{array}
\]

In total there are \(1+3+6+6=16\) ordered triples.  Hence the answer is

\[
\boxed{16}.
\]

First rewrite the divisibility in an equivalent symmetric form.  Observe

\[
\frac{(ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)}{abc}
=\frac{ABC+AB+BC+CA+A+B+C+1}{abc}
\quad\text{with }A=ab,B=bc,C=ca.
\]

One checks after simplification that

\[
\frac{(ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1)}{abc}
\;=\;
abc\;+\;(a+b+c)\;+\;\Bigl(\tfrac1a+\tfrac1b+\tfrac1c\Bigr)\;+\;\tfrac1{abc}
\;=\;
\underbrace{\bigl(\tfrac1a+\tfrac1b+\tfrac1c+\tfrac1{abc}\bigr)}_{k\in\Bbb Z}+(\,abc+a+b+c\,),
\]

so integrality is equivalent to

\[
\frac{bc+ca+ab+1}{abc}=k
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
bc+ca+ab+1=k\,abc,
\]
for some integer \(k\ge1\).

One then checks that the only positive‐integer solutions fall into four families, corresponding to \(k=4,3,2,1\), namely

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
k&\text{multiset }\{a,b,c\}&\text{number of orderings}\\\hline
4&\{1,1,1\}&1\\
3&\{1,1,2\}&3\\
2&\{1,2,3\}&6\\

\end{array}
\]

In total there are \(1+3+6+6=16\) ordered triples.  Hence the answer is

\[

\]
